title: Daily Market Digest: July 15, 2026
subtitle: Cooler Inflation and Strong Bank Earnings Drive Positive Sentiment Amid Geopolitical Concerns

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally on Soft Inflation Data and Robust Bank Earnings, Tech Leads Gains
items.summary: U.S. equities saw broad gains today, fueled by a cooler-than-expected June inflation report that tempered expectations for Federal Reserve rate hikes and strong quarterly results from major banks. Technology and semiconductor stocks led the advance, while geopolitical tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and elevated oil prices remain a key concern for investors.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Goldman Sachs surged 9% after reporting better-than-expected second-quarter earnings.

items.headline: SK Hynix Jumps on Semiconductor Momentum
items.summary: South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix jumped 9.4%, contributing to the strong performance of the semiconductor sector.

items.headline: Morgan Stanley Gains on Positive Trading and Fee Strength
items.summary: Morgan Stanley traded approximately 3% higher, driven by positive performance in equities trading and fee-based revenue.

items.headline: ASML Rallies on Strong AI Demand and Raised Outlook
items.summary: ASML Holding NV rose 3.40% in premarket trading after reporting blockbuster results and raising its annual guidance for the second time, citing unrelenting AI demand.

items.headline: Pentair Plunges on Guidance Cut and CFO Resignation
items.summary: Pentair PLC plunged 16.93% in premarket trading after slashing its guidance for fiscal year 2026 and announcing the resignation of its CFO.

items.headline: Wells Fargo Falls Amid Deposit Competition Concerns
items.summary: Wells Fargo fell around 3% after its earnings call highlighted intensifying competition for deposits and higher funding costs.

items.headline: Citigroup Drops on Funding Cost Worries
items.summary: Citigroup dropped approximately 5% as earnings discussions shifted focus to funding costs and operating leverage challenges.

items.headline: IBM Rebounds After Prior Session's Plunge
items.summary: IBM shares rebounded 1.19% in pre-market trading today after plunging 25% in the previous session due to weaker-than-expected preliminary results and client pauses in software and mainframe spending amid cybersecurity concerns.

items.headline: June CPI Undershoots Expectations, Easing Fed Hike Concerns
items.summary: The U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) unexpectedly fell 0.4% month-over-month in June, bringing the annual inflation rate down to 3.5%. Core CPI, excluding volatile food and energy, also saw its first monthly decline in over six years, easing to 2.6% annually. This softer inflation data has largely quashed market speculation of a Federal Reserve rate hike in July.

items.headline: Bank Earnings Kick Off Strong Q2 Season
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season commenced with strong reports from major U.S. banks. JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America both beat earnings estimates, with JPMorgan Chase rising 2.5% and Bank of America surging 1.9%. Goldman Sachs was a standout gainer, surging 9%. PNC Financial also reported record revenue and increased its quarterly dividend.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Elevate Oil Prices and Market Caution
items.summary: Oil prices remain elevated today as U.S.-Iran hostilities persist, leading to the reinstatement of a naval blockade near Iran's ports in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude traded marginally up at $85.23-$85.52 a barrel, while WTI crude rose to $79.95-$79.81. This geopolitical uncertainty continues to weigh on overall market sentiment.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductor Sectors Lead Market Rally
items.summary: Technology stocks, particularly semiconductors, are leading today's market rally. Micron Technology and Nvidia saw significant gains, rising 4.9% and 4.1% respectively. ASML Holding's robust earnings and raised outlook due to strong AI demand further underscore the strength in the semiconductor industry, despite recent volatility in chip stocks.

items.headline: Johnson & Johnson Raises 2026 Outlook on Strong Q2 Results
items.summary: Johnson & Johnson ($JNJ) reported strong second-quarter 2026 results, with reported sales growth of 6.6% to $25.3 billion and adjusted EPS of $2.90. The company raised its full-year 2026 guidance, now expecting over $100 billion in annual revenue. Innovative Medicine operational sales grew 6.8%, while MedTech sales increased 3.6%.
